title: lycopodium clavatum var. serpens
intro: Lycopodium clavatum var. serpens, also known as creeping club moss, is a plant species belonging to the Lycopodiaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots of Lycopodium clavatum var. serpens are typically used to make tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
